title.purpose: The single popup header line — the largest text in the UI at 15px.

body.purpose: Form input values, button labels, modal copy.

label.purpose: Uppercase field labels above every input.

caption.purpose: Auto-detect badges, toast text, modal meta line.

shadows.name: button-hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Primary submit button hover state, paired with a 1px translateY lift. Recolored from navy to the new terracotta primary.

shadows.name: modal-elevation
shadows.purpose: Duplicate-resolution modal box, the only true overlay in the system. Recolored from neutral black to the new warm ink.

motion.name: button-lift-transition
motion.purpose: Primary button hover lift and fill recolor.

motion.name: toast-fade
motion.purpose: Toast show/hide; no slide, fade only.

components.name: Header Brand Mark
components.description: Briefcase-and-checkmark icon mark paired with the popup title — the same glyph used for the toolbar/extension icon, carried into the UI for brand consistency.
components.html: Job Application Logger
components.css: .ds-header-brand { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-header-icon { width: 18px; height: 18px; color: #ba501c; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-header-brand h1 { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; color: #211815; }

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Full-width flat terracotta submit action; the extension's main CTA. No longer a gradient.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { width: 100%; padding: 11px; background: #ba501c; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.1s, box-shadow 0.1s, background 0.1s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #983000; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(186, 80, 28, 0.3); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: White bordered action, used for the modal's 'Log Anyway' choice.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { padding: 10px; background: #ffffff; color: #3f3632; border: 1px solid #dfd5d1; border-radius: 7px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #faf3f1; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Disconnect / destructive action inside the settings panel. Disables when nothing is connected.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { width: 100%; padding: 8px; background: #ffffff; color: #a50013; border: 1px solid #f97770; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-danger:hover:not(:disabled) { background: #ffe6e3; } .ds-btn-danger:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Standard text input used across the job-detail form.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 8px 10px; border: 1px solid #dfd5d1; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 13px; background: #ffffff; color: #211815; transition: border-color 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #ba501c; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(186, 80, 28, 0.15); }

components.name: Auto-Detect Badge
components.description: Inline pill next to a field label showing whether a value was auto-detected from the job page. Uses the same green as the confirmed/connected family.
components.css: .ds-badge-auto { display: inline-block; background: #ddf6e4; color: #00582c; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 600; padding: 1px 5px; border-radius: 3px; margin-left: 5px; vertical-align: middle; } .ds-badge-auto--missing { background: #faf3f1; color: #766863; }

components.name: Semantic Banner
components.description: Tinted, bordered status banner — warning/error/success variants, the system's primary state-communication device. "Connected" now shares the success/green family rather than its own blue.
components.css: .ds-banner { border-radius: 8px; padding: 9px 12px; font-size: 13px; } .ds-banner--warning { background: #fcedcd; border: 1px solid #d4a73e; color: #6d4400; } .ds-banner--error { background: #ffe6e3; border: 1px solid #f97770; color: #a50013; } .ds-banner--success { background: #ddf6e4; border: 1px solid #68b986; color: #00582c; }

components.name: Toast
components.css: .ds-toast { position: fixed; bottom: 14px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); background: #211815; color: #ffffff; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 20px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .ds-toast.visible { opacity: 1; }

narrative.overview: The redesign replaces the generic navy-gradient-on-slate look (the prior baseline, now retired) with a warm terracotta accent on a neutral, near-white ground — the palette of a well-kept field instrument, not a SaaS dashboard. The extension's whole job is to take something that just happened (reading a job posting) and stamp it into permanent record; the visual system leans into that with a literal checkmark-through-a-briefcase mark carried from the toolbar icon into the popup header, and a single warm terracotta used sparingly for exactly the actions that 'confirm' something (the submit button, the primary modal action, focus rings) while everything else stays a calm, warm-tinted neutral. This is explicitly what the interface should NOT look like: another gradient-header B2B tool, another blue-accent dashboard, another pastel-banner-for-every-state template — the previous system named all three by name as anti-references, and this system replaces every instance of them.
keyCharacteristics: One committed brand color (terracotta) carrying roughly 5-10% of the surface — the submit button, the header/toolbar icon mark, focus rings, and the primary modal action. Restrained, not decorative.

Confirmation states (auto-detected fields, the connected-to-Sheets banner, the success banner) all share one green, unifying "this is good, this is confirmed" into a single hue instead of splitting blue/green as the old system did.
Warm-tinted neutrals throughout (bg, surface, border, ink all carry a faint chroma toward the terracotta hue) instead of the old cool slate-gray family — subtle cohesion, not a "warm theme."
Flat by default; the only shadows are the submit-button hover lift and the duplicate modal's overlay, unchanged from the prior system's discipline.
System font stack, no display type — unchanged; the popup format still has no room for a hero moment.

rules.name: The One Brand Color Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is the only color that means "brand" or "primary action." Every other color in the system is semantic (success/warning/error) or structural (the neutral ramp). If a new element needs emphasis, reach for weight or space before reaching for a second brand color.

rules.name: The One Confirmation Color Rule
rules.body: Auto-detected, connected, and successfully-saved all render in the same green. Don't reintroduce a separate blue for "connected" — that was the old system's split vocabulary, and this system deliberately unified it.

rules.name: The No-Display-Type Rule
rules.body: There is no heading larger than 15px anywhere in this system — the popup format has no room for a hero moment, and the current design doesn't try to manufacture one.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Nothing casts a shadow until it's either hovered or is the modal. A settings panel, a banner, a form field — all flat, bordered, at rest.

dos: Do keep terracotta to the handful of "primary action" moments (submit, primary modal button, focus ring, icon mark) — per the One Brand Color Rule.
Do keep "connected," "auto-detected," and "success" all in the same green family — per the One Confirmation Color Rule.
Do keep the warm-tinted neutral ramp (bg/surface/border/ink all carry a faint terracotta chroma) rather than reverting to a cool gray scale.
donts: Don't reintroduce a gradient on the header or any button — the flat terracotta fill is deliberate, not a placeholder.
Don't add a second, unrelated brand color "for variety." One accent, used consistently, is the point.
Don't let the DEV-mode badge drift back to an arbitrary bright yellow — it's part of the warning family now, meaningfully, not just decoratively colored.
